behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction specifies all constant names, function names, parameter names, marker strings ('... [truncated from {original_len} chars]'), summary keys ('_truncated', '_message'), metadata keys ('_response_truncated', '_truncation_notes'), and depth limit 10.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the required API surface and phases but leaves implementation details (how to iterate, how to check limit) to the agent.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on documented API (function names, markers, return tuple shape) all of which are specified in instruction.md, so alternate correct implementations would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests import functions and call them, checking return values and data mutations.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests verify specific behavior like truncation markers, list length reductions, and note contents that stubs cannot satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover INFO_TOOLS, each helper, orchestrator, and (per truncated visible portion) integration; visible tests align with instruction requirements.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped into well-named classes (TestTruncateStrings, TestSummarizeLargeDicts, etc.) with docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Summary dict schema ('_truncated', '_message') and metadata keys are explicitly documented.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names target files (token_utils.py, middleware.py) which are the intended edit locations, not leaking specific buggy lines.
